---
title: "Rendering"
---

Rendering applications are scaffolded. They should remain exploratory until benchmarks, papers, and manifests specify stronger claims.

<div class="warning-box">Rendering demos are not proof artifacts.</div>

The current proved seed for rendering-adjacent work is the finite direction-bin schedule used by the CoilRay and sampling roadmap. It proves address-safety and repeated-bin closure only; it does not prove ray coherence, lower noise, or better frame time.

::: {.learning-unit}

## Case-Study Frame

The circular address space is a finite set of direction bins. The repeated action is stepping through bins by a stride. This can explain safe schedules, but visual quality and performance remain benchmark questions.

:::

## What To Notice

- The proved seed covers bin indexing and closure.
- Rendering quality claims need images, metrics, and baselines.
- A demo can be useful for intuition without being a proof artifact.

::: {.checkpoint-box}

## Checkpoint

What extra evidence would be needed before claiming a direction-bin schedule improves rendering output?

:::

## Source Trail

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="COMPR-T0001"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="COMPR-T0004"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="COMPR-T0005"></div>

<p data-paper-ref="papers/applications/PAPER_COMP_02_COIL_RAY_AND_SAMPLING.md">Paper source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/applications/PAPER_COMP_02_COIL_RAY_AND_SAMPLING.md">Coil Ray And Sampling</a></p>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
